title: 'It was quite a light show!' NASA astronaut spies dramatic fireball from the International Space Station (photos)
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a dramatic fireball from the International Space Station. The event was described as a stunning light show from above.

title: Spaceflight is hard on the heart, yet artificial ones grow better in space than on Earth
summary: Researchers found that mini-hearts grown from human stem cells develop significantly faster in space than on Earth, despite the negative effects of spaceflight on the human heart. This discovery could advance tissue engineering and regenerative medicine.

title: New this month, this Lego Star Wars Yoda bust would terrify Darth Vader and I have to have it
summary: A new Lego Star Wars Yoda bust has been released, praised for its impressive design despite past issues with larger models.

title: Why do some stars become 'supernova impostors'? Astronomers still don't quite know
summary: Astronomers are studying 'supernova impostors,' stars that exhibit eruptive mass loss, a phenomenon not yet fully understood. This stellar behavior remains a mystery in astrophysics.

title: New for Star Wars Day, grab this Lego Star Wars model of The Mandalorian's coolest ship and get the Darksaber for free
summary: Lego released a new Star Wars UCS set featuring The Mandalorian's N-1 starfighter, with a free Darksaber model for a limited time.

title: SpaceX sends 45 satellites to orbit in nighttime launch from California (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 45 satellites into orbit from California early Sunday morning, including a South Korean spacecraft that was delayed since 2022.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 208 — Lander, Lander, Who's Got a Lander
summary: Episode 208 of the This Week In Space podcast discusses the risks and realities of the new space race to the moon with Space.com's Mike Wall as a guest.

title: From new releases to classic models, feel the force this Star Wars Day with the best Lego sets from the saga
summary: Lego is launching new Star Wars-themed sets for Star Wars Day, highlighting both new releases and classic models.

title: NASA Kennedy Center Director Announces Plans to Retire
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center Director Janet Petro announced her retirement. She began her career at NASA in 2007 and worked her way up to center director.

title: NASA Artemis II Crew Rings Nasdaq Closing Bell
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew rang the Nasdaq closing bell to celebrate the mission, which includes astronauts from NASA and the Canadian Space Agency. The event was attended by Nasdaq's CEO Adena T. Friedman and highlighted the mission's significance.

title: Key Support Equipment Arrives at Kennedy for Roman Space Telescope
summary: NASA received key support equipment, including HEPA wall modules, for the Roman Space Telescope at Kennedy Space Center's Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility. The equipment will aid in launch processing for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope.

title: NASA’s STORIE Mission to Tell Tale of Earth’s Ring Current
summary: NASA's STORIE mission aims to study Earth's ring current, a doughnut-shaped region of charged particles trapped by Earth's magnetic field. This research will help understand space weather and its effects on Earth.

title: Space Out This Summer with Variety of NASA STEM Activities
summary: NASA has announced a variety of STEM activities for summer, offering creative, outdoor, and educational opportunities for the public. These initiatives aim to engage people of all ages in space exploration and learning.

title: Hubble Spots a Starry Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy NGC 3137, located 53 million light-years away in the constellation Antlia. This galaxy provides astronomers with a valuable opportunity to study the lifecycle of stars.

title: Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides
summary: Heavy rains from Tropical Cyclone Maila caused a deadly landslide in Papua New Guinea's East New Britain region. The disaster highlights the severe weather risks in the area.

title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
summary: Ireland is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington on May 4. The event will be hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and attended by Irish officials.

title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission celebrated its 25th launch anniversary on April 15, 2026, with a team reunion and the unveiling of a global Mars map created using THEMIS imagery.

title: What’s Up: May 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for May 2026, including meteor showers, a Moon-Venus conjunction, and a rare blue moon. The event highlights celestial events visible to the naked eye.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight with a revised trajectory. The return of Starship launches is expected within weeks.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. The launch took place from the Baikonur Cosmodrome.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and military service overseas.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks the return of the heavy-lift vehicle to active service.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the successful Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites in six launches across Florida, California, and French Guiana. This marks the return of Falcon Heavy and highlights multiple international launch activities.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, potentially indicating ancient life. The findings mark a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship's Block 3 at the Cape, aiming for a launch this year. The first flight of Block 3 is anticipated as the company advances its development.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates milestones across ESA's missions and contributions to space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing procedure for a space mission, involving high-temperature exposure to simulate re-entry conditions. This test is essential for ensuring the safety and reliability of the spacecraft's landing system.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life while adhering to planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly improve satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming it is on track for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process, including an SME Forum in June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris. The event follows an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was highlighted as ideal for navigation purposes.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses and collected over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data since July 2025. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear industry executives are enthusiastic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which Administrator Isaacman views as a key step in the agency's evolution. NASA is significantly supporting this initiative beyond just funding.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion NASA budget, opposing the administration's FY27 request. This marks the first legislative step to reject the proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms secured €1.4 billion in private capital in 2025, indicating growing investor confidence in the sector. This trend reflects increasing comfort among private European markets in funding space industry startups.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has secured £9.8 million in seed funding to develop cancer drugs in microgravity, aiming to reformulate treatments for self-administration by patients. The company leverages space-based manufacturing to improve drug delivery and reduce reliance on medical staff.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST introduced a new satellite product designed to visualize urban development changes, helping construction firms, insurers, and city authorities assess risks and consequences of construction projects.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance multi-domain capabilities.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The competitive landscape for orbital data centers includes major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Sophia Space, along with satellite manufacturers such as Apex and K2 Space. The post highlights the growing interest and investment in space-based data infrastructure.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully hosted Liquid AI's large language model in orbit to analyze an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The demo, part of the Clustergate-2 mission launched on Momentus' Vigoride 7 in March, marks a new era in space-based AI processing.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon stated that the Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, implying no unique differentiators compared to existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump's decision to cut the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it harmful to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Lynk, EarthDaily, True Anomaly Launch Satellites on Weekend SpaceX Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX launched 45 payloads, including satellites from Lynk, EarthDaily, and True Anomaly, on a rideshare mission primarily carrying a South Korean Earth Observation satellite. The mission took place early Sunday morning.

title: Loft to Build 10 EO Satellites for French Constellation With Magellium Artal Group
summary: Loft will construct 10 Earth Observation (EO) satellites for a French constellation in collaboration with Magellium Artal Group. This partnership marks a significant step in France's next-generation EO satellite initiative.

title: Orbital Warfare Systems Top Priority for Space Chief Nominee Lt. Gen. Schiess
summary: Orbital warfare systems are the top priority for the White House’s nominee to lead the U.S. Space Force, Lt. Gen. Schiess. The focus on enhancing orbital warfare capabilities comes as a key objective under the Trump administration.

title: Sateliot and Telefónica España to Explore 5G Satellite Connectivity for Defense, Military Uses
summary: Sateliot and Telefónica España have partnered to explore 5G satellite connectivity for defense, military, and remote area applications using Sateliot's satellite IoT technology.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions for network resiliency at Modern Day Marine. The conversation highlighted Lite Coms' product designs aimed at enhancing communication resilience.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. The collaboration aims to enhance frequency management efficiency.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space for $355 million to create a complete communications ecosystem. The deal aims to enhance York's capabilities in satellite communications.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC has adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses longstanding concerns about interference and coordination in satellite communications.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as it transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming launch and its implications in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) has announced an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move aims to enhance technological capabilities in the space sector.
